focus: Q1: Undocumented features -- source tree vs feature catalog cross-reference
answeredQuestions: Q1: All 32 MCP tools cataloged; ~25-35 infrastructure/cross-cutting source files NOT cataloged (api/, formatters/, core/, utils/, lib/errors/, lib/utils/)

focus: Q2: Unaudited code paths -- file-level cross-reference of 286 source files vs 254 catalog references
answeredQuestions: Q2: 32 of 286 source files (11.2%) never referenced by any catalog entry. 6 CRITICAL, 9 HIGH, 8 MEDIUM, 5 LOW, 4 INFORMATIONAL.

focus: Q3: Verify PARTIAL audit findings for accuracy -- spot-check corrections against source code
answeredQuestions: Q3: PARTIAL findings detect real discrepancies but ~50% of audit corrections contain errors (fabricated file/function names). MATCH boundary may also be unreliable.

focus: Q4: Cross-feature blind spots -- shared module import analysis vs catalog coverage
answeredQuestions: Q4: 4 active production modules have ZERO catalog mentions despite core handler imports. hooks/ system (17 importers), session-manager (41KB monolith), and 3 cognitive modules (~30KB) are structural blind spots. Per-category audit cannot verify cross-cutting shared modules.

focus: Q5: Temporal gaps -- code changes during and after the audit window
answeredQuestions: Q5: 234 of 286 .ts files (82%) modified during audit window across 27 commits. 27 new source files added, 6 uncataloged. Zero post-audit drift. Audit ran concurrently with 4 implementation waves.

focus: Q6: Feature flag graduation impact on audit validity
answeredQuestions: Q6: 22 flags graduated from opt-in to default-ON mid-audit (Mar 21). Behavioral semantics reversed (undefined=ENABLED). Phase 020 audited only 7 of 40+ flags. Audit used moving HEAD baseline, not pinned SHA.

focus: Q7: Test files covering undocumented functionality -- test-catalog alignment gap
answeredQuestions: Q7: Only 9 of 321 test files (2.8%) referenced in catalog. 312 test files exercise undocumented behaviors: 21 regression/safety/security, 31 scoring/fusion, 14 handler, 9 integration, 22 memory-specific. 97.2% gap is structural (features vs behaviors).

focus: Q8: Quantitative per-phase risk model synthesizing all gap dimensions
keyQuestions: Q9: audit agent correction reliability pattern
answeredQuestions: Q8: 6-dimension composite risk model built for all 21 phases. Phase 011 (scoring, 0.80) and 014 (pipeline, 0.78) are CRITICAL. 5 phases HIGH risk. Top 2 phases concentrate 20% features + 31% unreferenced files. 5 systemic remediation recommendations produced.

focus: Q9 hallucination verification + Q10 session-manager blind spot
answeredQuestions: Q9: PARTIAL correction accuracy 85.7% (18/21 verifiable), revises iter 3 ~50% estimate downward
Q10: session-manager 85% functions unaudited, confirmed structural blind spot, mitigated by 3 test files

Q11: 3-tier re-audit plan (27-38 hr): systemic S1+S2 (pin SHA + cross-cutting audit), 7 targeted re-audits T1-T7, 3 new categories N1-N3. All 7 at-risk phases drop below 0.50. Consolidated 10 iterations into 5 core findings + 1 action plan.
